mutators.name: registerAnimal
mutators.semantics: Appends an AnimalRegistered event and returns its animal handle; draft requires one animal_type taxonomy reference (an unknown supplied term name is created/reused as an animal_type term), accepts only sex code 'F' or 'M', retains nickname values in supplied order, and stores only canonical isSterile values plus any supplied parent handles.

mutators.name: recordBirth
mutators.semantics: Appends one atomic BirthRecorded event for its children, optional mother, and occurrence data; locally rejects a child already claimed by another visible birth, while independently created remote claims are retained for deterministic convergence rather than globally rejected.

mutators.name: correctBirthRecord
mutators.semantics: Appends a BirthCorrected event that supersedes the named birth's prior birth fields/child claims for later projections and reruns the same derived maternal-lineage and per-child uniqueness rules.

mutators.name: migrateLegacyCastrationRows
mutators.semantics: Appends canonical is_sterile migration events for every supplied legacy is_castrated row, preserving its animal, legacy revision, language, delta, and boolean value; no canonical is_castrated field is emitted.
mutators.derived_from: invented: the historical is_castrated-to-is_sterile field-row migration must be representable as replayable events, but no single listed source method names that migration.

projections.name: getAnimalLifecycle
projections.semantics: Projects the animal at or before asOf: its required AnimalTypeRef, sex code, ordered nickname array, canonical sterility values (including preserved revision/language/delta migration contexts), parent handles, and rendered birthSummary; a birth mother is appended to a child only when that child had no parent handle, while any existing parent list is an unchanged complete veto.

projections.name: getBirthRecord
projections.semantics: Projects the latest effective version of a birth at or before asOf, including its mother, occurrence data, and each claimed child marked accepted or displaced; for every child with competing synchronized birth claims, the lexicographically smallest BirthHandle is the sole accepted canonical birth and all other claims are reported as conflicts.

projections.name: getBirthForAnimal
projections.semantics: Returns the one deterministically accepted birth summary for the animal at or before asOf, or null when none is accepted; concurrent duplicate birth logs converge to the UUID/handle-tiebreak winner rather than assuming a globally serialized uniqueness gate.
projections.derived_from: invented: the source exposes birth information through entity hooks and themed asset rendering rather than a single canonical-birth query; the local-first port needs this projection to expose the eventual uniqueness resolution.

rationale: Animal registration is a domain event rather than a generic asset write and carries the field-definition semantics: required auto-creatable animal_type taxonomy reference, closed F/M codes, ordered multi-value nicknames, canonical is_sterile, and parent references. Birth recording/correction is modeled as an atomic composite event because source insert/update hooks derive child lineage and the target has no multi-write transaction. Animal lifecycle projection exposes that derived lineage and the ThemeHooks birth-facing value. The server-only UniqueBirthLog invariant is adapted to the target profile by replica-local rejection plus deterministic UUID/handle winner selection after sync; losing claims remain observable as conflicts. Legacy castration migration is explicit so every old field-row identity survives as canonical sterility event data.
